description:
You are an on–call operations engineer who has just received an alert saying that several application-critical Docker containers are no longer healthy.  
To start your triage you find that a colleague has already copied the current output of

    docker ps --all --no-trunc

into the text file  

    /home/user/incident/docker_ps.out

Your job is to generate a short, strictly formatted report that lists every *unhealthy* container (anything whose STATUS does **not** begin with the word “Up”).  
Create the report in the file

    /home/user/incident/unhealthy_containers.log

The report **must follow these exact rules** so that our automated checker can grade your work:

1. The report is plain UTF-8 text (no colour codes, no extra spaces at line ends).  
2. Line 1 is the literal, fixed timestamp shown below – do **not** generate the current time:  
   2024-01-01T00:00:00Z  
   (That static timestamp makes automated testing easier.)  
3. Starting on line 2, list one unhealthy container per line, in the same order in which they appear in docker_ps.out.  
   Each line has the format:  
   <container-name> | <STATUS-column-verbatim‐as-it-appears-in-docker_ps.out>  
   Example (this is exactly what the grading script will expect for the sample data that is already in docker_ps.out):  
       redis-cache | Exited (1) 30 minutes ago  
4. There must be a final trailing newline character at the end of the file.

Hints (do **not** show commands):  
• “Healthy” containers always have STATUS that starts with the word “Up”.  
• Everything else (“Exited …”, “Restarting …”, etc.) counts as unhealthy.  
• Only the STATUS column text and the NAMES column text are needed for the report.  
• Tools such as awk, grep, cut, or sed are usually enough to extract the required fields.

When you are finished, /home/user/incident/unhealthy_containers.log should exist and exactly match the required format. Automated tests will compare the file byte-for-byte, so please be precise.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the task starts:

Path: /home/user/incident/docker_ps.out  
Contents (including the header line and exact spacing between columns):

CONTAINER ID   IMAGE          COMMAND                  CREATED        STATUS                       PORTS                   NAMES
1a2b3c4d5e6f   nginx:1.25     "/docker-entrypoint.…"   2 hours ago    Up 2 hours (healthy)         0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp    web-frontend
7e8f9a0b1c2d   redis:7.0      "docker-entrypoint.s…"   3 hours ago    Exited (1) 30 minutes ago                            redis-cache
3d4e5f6a7b8c   postgres:15    "docker-entrypoint.s…"   3 hours ago    Restarting (1) 5 seconds ago 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp db-primary

Required file after successful task completion:

Path: /home/user/incident/unhealthy_containers.log  
Exact contents (including the final newline character):

2024-01-01T00:00:00Z
redis-cache | Exited (1) 30 minutes ago
db-primary | Restarting (1) 5 seconds ago

(The grading script will open unhealthy_containers.log and compare its full byte sequence to the text shown above.)
name: task_000000_cf69fe1f
